name: Black Bean Soup (Aztec or Yucateca Style)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the black beans and soak them overnight with 1 teaspoon of salt and a green bell pepper.
2. In a 6-quart stewpan, bring the beans, soaking water, and reserved pepper to a boil.
3. Simmer until tender (really tender).
4. Heat the oil in a deep frying pan.
5. Sauté onion, garlic, and green chile until soft.
6. Remove 2 1/2 cups of bean broth from the pot and add it to the frying pan. Simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Strain onions, garlic, and peppers from the broth and discard them.
8. Discard the bell pepper.
9. Add seasoned broth back to the soup pot.
10. Add all remaining ingredients except vinegar and olive oil to the soup pot and simmer for 1 1/2 hours until thick. If too thick, add water.
11. Add vinegar and olive oil before serving.
